assertions.text: Driver braked hard for approximately four to five seconds before the collision.
Vehicle speed before braking was about 60 km/h.
Braking occurred well ahead of the collision (i.e., the brake application began before impact).
The vehicle struck the object/other party (or was struck) in a forward direction during the collision.
Despite heavy braking from about 60 km/h, the collision was not avoided and the vehicle came to a stop as a result of the impact.
After braking and impact, the vehicle came to a complete stop.

verdicts.evidence: [Brake was applied for multiple seconds ending before impact as gps_speed shows a gradual drop from 63.0 km/h to 60.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=6.0 (T2) and a sustained 60.0 km/h at t=[5.0,6.0] followed by deceleration at t=7.0 to 52.0 km/h (T3). The accel integrated delta-v on accel_x over the window is [−51.0 km/h](T2) indicating prolonged deceleration rather than a brief tap. The gps_speed samples show speed values of [60.0](T4) at t=5.0 and [60.0](T4) at t=6.0 and then [52.0](T4) at t=7.0 consistent with braking beginning before impact) .

[The first recorded gps_speed sample is 63.0 km/h at t=0.0 and values of 62.0 and 61.0 follow, with 60.0 km/h recorded at t=4.0–6.0 (T3), supporting the statement that speed before braking was about 60 km/h].

[Brake-related deceleration is evident before the impact spike: gps_speed drops from 60.0 km/h at t=6.0 to 52.0 km/h at t=7.0 and further thereafter (T4), while high-magnitude IMU peaks occur around t=7.44 s (accel_x peak at [3.953 g at t=7.44](T2) and gyro_y peak at [360.514 deg/s at t=7.44](T2)), indicating the brake application (speed decrease) began before the impact spike].

[The IMU axes are device-frame and alignment to vehicle longitudinal axis is undocumented (T1), and while accel_x shows a peak of [3.953 g at t=7.44](T2) and gps_speed falls (T3), the direction of impact relative to the vehicle forward direction cannot be determined from the available telemetry alone].

[Sensor data show a single dominant crash pulse: accel_x peak_abs_dev is [3.953 g at t=7.44](T2) with gyro peaks clustered around t≈7.4 s (T2), and no separate secondary impact pulses are evident in the time series from t=5–9 s (gps_speed and IMU samples show one major spike cluster at t≈7.4 in T4), consistent with a single collision].

[The vehicle decelerated from about 63.0 km/h first recorded (T2) and gps_speed falls from [60.0 km/h at t=6.0](T4) to [52.0 km/h at t=7.0](T4) then to [35.0 km/h at t=8.0 and 19.0 km/h at t=9.0](T3); the accel integrated delta-v on accel_x is [−51.0 km/h](T2), and the last gps_speed sample in the window is [0.0 km/h at t=16.0](T2), showing the impact was not avoided and the vehicle came to a stop as a result].
[The gps_speed channel first_value is [63.0 km/h at t=0.0] and last_value is [0.0 km/h at t=16.0] (T2), indicating the vehicle came to a complete stop after the event].

result.rationale: Telemetry shows prolonged braking beginning before a single, strong impact pulse near t≈7.4 s, with speed decreasing from about 60–63 km/h and falling to zero by t=16 s; IMU and GPS data align to support the narrative except for impact direction which is unverifiable from device-frame axes.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
